manifest.name: Say

name: Say

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Say 0.1.1

Security hardening and dependency patch release.

### Fixes

- **Shell execution hardening** — reject control characters in TTS text and shell metacharacters in command names; use `spawn` with argument arrays (no shell interpretation) and `stdio: "ignore"` to prevent injection
- **fast-uri vulnerability patched** — override transitive dev-dependency `fast-uri` to `>=3.1.2`, resolving path-traversal (GHSA-q3j6-qgpj-74h6) and host-confusion (GHSA-v39h-62p7-jpjc) CVEs

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Say 0.1.0

Initial release of the Say plugin for Obsidian.

### What it does

Speaks the first H1 heading of a linked note when you hover over an internal link, using your system's TTS engine.

### Features

- **Cross-platform TTS** — works with `say` (macOS), `spd-say` (Linux), `espeak`, and PowerShell `SpeechSynthesizer` (Windows)
- **Smart heading resolution** — reads the first H1; falls back to the file name
- **Debounced hover** — only speaks after the mouse dwells on a link for a configurable delay (default 300 ms)
- **Custom voice and rate** — adjustable via settings
- **Custom TTS command** — override the default engine with your own command and args template

### Bug fixes since initial submission

- Fixed `activeWindow.setTimeout`/`clearTimeout` for popout window compatibility
- Removed disallowed `eslint-disable` directive comments
- Fixed release workflow manifest version mismatch
- Added artifact attestations to release assets
